title: The Empress
content:
Li Wei spent the next few days working on the memorial for her father. She met with architects and artisans, discussing designs and materials. It was a lot of work, but Li Wei enjoyed it. It gave her a sense of purpose.

One afternoon, while she was reviewing the plans, the Empress summoned her.

"Lady Li," the Empress said, motioning for her to sit. "I've heard about your project."

"Yes, Empress," Li Wei said. "I'm building a memorial for my father."

The Empress nodded. "That's a noble cause. But I have a question for you."

"What is it, Empress?"

"Why did the Emperor choose you for this task?" the Empress asked. "There are many other people in the palace who could have done it."

Li Wei hesitated. "I'm not sure, Empress."

The Empress smiled, but it was cold. "I think I know why," she said. "He's testing you. He wants to see if you're worthy of his favor."

She leaned forward, her voice low. "And let me tell you something, Lady Li. If you fail, you will regret it."

Li Wei nodded. "I understand, Empress."

***

As Li Wei left the Empress's quarters, she felt a sense of foreboding. The Empress was clearly threatened by her, and she would not hesitate to eliminate her if she got in the way.

That night, Li Wei had a dream. She was back in her father's study, and he was standing before her.

"Wei'er," he said, his voice gentle. "You must be strong. The palace is dangerous, but you have the strength to survive."

"Father," Li Wei said, tears in her eyes. "I miss you."

Her father smiled. "I'm always with you, Wei'er. Remember that."

He reached out and touched her face, then vanished.

Li Wei woke up, her face wet with tears. But she felt stronger, more determined.

She would honor her father's memory, and she would survive in the palace.

***

The next day, Li Wei continued working on the memorial. She was walking through the palace gardens when she saw the Emperor talking to a group of ministers.

"Your Majesty," Li Wei said, bowing.

The Emperor looked up and smiled. "Lady Li! Come and join us."

Li Wei walked over, her heart racing. She had never spoken to the Emperor in front of other people before.

"This is Lady Li," the Emperor said, introducing her to the ministers. "She's overseeing the memorial for her father, General Li."

The ministers nodded, their expressions neutral. But Li Wei could see the curiosity in their eyes.

"Lady Li," one of the ministers said. "I heard that your father was a great general."

"Yes," Li Wei said. "He was."

Another minister spoke up. "And what do you think of the current state of our army?"

Li Wei hesitated, then spoke. "I think our army is strong, but we need to be prepared for any threat. My father always said that the best defense is a good offense."

The ministers nodded, impressed by her answer.

The Emperor smiled. "Well said, Lady Li."

As Li Wei walked away, she could feel the eyes of the ministers on her. She had made an impression, and that was both good and bad.

Good, because it meant that the Emperor was noticing her. Bad, because it meant that she was becoming a target.

But Li Wei didn't care. She was determined to make her father proud, and she would do whatever it took to survive.

The game was just beginning, and she was ready to play.
